title: Smart Commit: Training seed policy
original_text: Updated the local ML training maintenance workflow. train_model.py now records whether built-in seed examples were included, supports --no-seed for dataset-only experiments, and the documentation explains when to regenerate commit_model.pkl, vectorizer.pkl, and model_metadata.json.
expected_body_lines: - Record whether seed examples were included in metadata
- Document when to regenerate model artifacts
- Keep built-in seed examples as temporary support data
expected_command: git commit -m "chore(ml): document training seed policy"
